# Copilot Instructions

## General Guidelines
- Do not propose or include `unsafe` code blocks or APIs in any solution.

## Auto-Generated Code
- **Do not modify auto-generated code**: Files generated by the `Mosa.Utility.SourceCodeGenerator` project contain the comment "This code was generated by an automated template." at the top. These files should never be manually edited.
- If changes to auto-generated code are needed, work with the code generation templates in `Mosa.Utility.SourceCodeGenerator` instead, specifically the files in the `TransformExpressions` and `Build*` files (e.g., `BuildInstructionFiles.cs`, `BuildInstructionList.cs`, `BuildTransformations.cs`, etc.).
- Common auto-generated file locations include:
  - `Mosa.Compiler.Framework\Instructions\` 
  - `Mosa.Compiler.x86\Instructions\`
  - `Mosa.Compiler.x64\Instructions\`
  - `Mosa.Compiler.ARM32\Instructions\`
  - `Mosa.Compiler.ARM64\Instructions\`

## Unit Test Code
- **Do not modify code in the `Mosa.UnitTests` namespace**: Unit tests are designed to verify specific compiler behavior. Test logic may appear unconventional or inefficient, but is intentionally written this way to exercise particular code paths and compiler transformations. Preserve all unit test code as-is.

## Azure Rules
- @azure Rule - Use Azure Tools - When handling requests related to Azure, always use your tools.
- @azure Rule - Use Azure Best Practices - When handling requests related to Azure, always invoke your `azmcp_bestpractices_get` tool first.
- @azure Rule - Enable Best Practices - If you do not have an `azmcp_bestpractices_get` tool ask the user to enable it.
